summary: The CI job failed while attempting to push a new branch to GitHub. The git push command received a 403 Forbidden error, indicating the workflow lacked sufficient permissions to push to the repository.
root_cause: The git push to 'https://github.com/vercel/next.js/' failed with HTTP 403, meaning the GitHub token used by the workflow does not have write permission to push the branch 'update/react/19.3.0-canary-142cfde8-20260422'.

evidence.quote: fatal: unable to access 'https://github.com/vercel/next.js/': The requested URL returned error: 403
evidence.reason: Explicit HTTP 403 from GitHub when attempting the push, indicating an authentication/authorization failure.

evidence.quote: Error: Command failed with exit code 128 (Unknown system error -128): git push origin update/react/19.3.0-canary-142cfde8-20260422
evidence.reason: Shows the exact git push command that failed, confirming the failure point is the remote push step.

suggested_fix: Ensure the GitHub Actions workflow token has 'contents: write' permission (add 'permissions: contents: write' to the workflow or job), or verify that the secret/PAT used for authentication is valid and has push access to the repository.

model_info._backfill_note: Pre-Codex-2026-05-14 F2 fix. The Claude shim now persists _model_info from every API call; this row predates that emit so resolved_model and usage are not recoverable. requested_model is set to the shim-level alias (haiku for v1, sonnet for v2) which is what the post-fix shim emits.
